Human.description: Balanced and adaptable. No particular strengths or weaknesses — masters of their own fate.

Elf.description: Agile and keen-minded, yet less hardy than most. Elves favour speed and intellect over brute force.

Dwarf.description: Stout and resilient warriors forged in mountain halls. Tough and strong, though slower and less social.
Dwarf.notes: Tough and strong, slower & less social

Lycanthrope.description: Cursed with beast blood, Lycanthropes are powerful and fast — but their primal nature dulls mind and foresight.
Lycanthrope.notes: Beastly instincts: strong & fast, low INT/WIS

Orc.description: Bred for war, Orcs are raw power embodied. Formidable warriors, but wisdom and wit are not their forte.

Gnome.description: Brilliant tinkerers and scholars, Gnomes excel at magic and puzzles — though their frail frames make battle perilous.

Tiefling.description: Born of infernal heritage, Tieflings weave charm and dark magic with ease. Their cursed blood, however, makes them less durable.

Dragonborn.description: Proud descendants of ancient dragons, Dragonborn are strong and honourable — formidable warriors though not the sharpest minds.
Dragonborn.notes: Strong and honourable, not super bright

Goblin.description: Cunning and lightning-quick, Goblins use wit and speed to outwit foes — though their scrawny frames leave them fragile.

Troll.description: Massive and near-unkillable, Trolls boast tremendous strength and endurance. In exchange, intellect and speed are far behind.
Troll.notes: Big, strong, regenerative, but dumb & slow
